question: Will Hezbollah of Lebanon be fully disarmed before June, 2026?
reason: Hezbollah's consistent rejection of disarmament plans and ongoing military activities make full disarmament by May 31, 2026, highly unlikely.
evidence: Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem has called on the Lebanese government to withdraw from direct talks with Israel, labeling them as concessions and advocating instead for indirect negotiations facilitated by a third party. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/462af0a3095db4b5a95f2898d1c5a3f4?utm_source=openai))

As of May 18, 2026, the death toll in the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has risen to 3,020, including 292 women and 211 children, according to Lebanon's health ministry. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/2d0737f122640d72b247bd9e6643b537?utm_source=openai))

On May 21, 2026, the U.S. imposed sanctions on Lebanese parliamentarians, security officials, and Hezbollah affiliates for allegedly supporting the Iran-backed militia’s influence over Lebanon’s state institutions and obstructing disarmament efforts. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/d654875131c303a387173de7dcad6815?utm_source=openai))
